---
title: "Copyright Office Seeks Public Input on AI Protections and Liability"
description: "Three open questions: where to draw the line, when training is infringing, and how to handle infringing AI outputs."
author: "synesis"
date: "2023-08-30"
categories: [AI, LLMs, NLP, generative AI, links]
image: cover.jpg
---

![](cover.jpg)

Copyright Office Seeks Public Input on AI Protections and Liability:

1. Where and how to draw the line between human creation and AI-generated content.

2. Whether and when the use of copyrighted materials to train AI models is infringing.

3. How copyright principles could be applied to infringing material created by AI systems.

[[1]](#ref-1)

(Previously: "US District Judge ruled an AI-generated artwork can't be copyrighted" [[2]](#ref-2))

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_copyright-office-seeks-public-input-on-ai-activity-7102681259151171584-eGPH).*

---

## References

<a id="ref-1"></a>[1] "Copyright Office Seeks Public Input on AI Protections and Liability." *Bloomberg Law*, 2023. <https://news.bloomberglaw.com/ip-law/copyright-office-seeks-public-input-on-ai-protections-liability>

<a id="ref-2"></a>[2] Benjamin Han. "AI-Generated Art Cannot Be Copyrighted, Rules a US Federal Judge." *synesis*, 2023. [Read on this blog.](../20230819-ai-art-copyright-ruling/index.html)
